title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a woman giving information to students at a language school.
audio_script: Woman: Good afternoon, everyone. I’m going to tell you about your first week at Lakeside Language School. Most of you are here to improve your English, but on Wednesday afternoon you can try another language. This week the extra class is [14]French[/14]. It’s only for beginners, so don’t worry if you know nothing about it. Your morning lessons will be in Room 6, but the afternoon activities usually start in the [15]library[/15], where there is more space. Please bring a [16]pencil[/16] to every lesson. Pens are fine for most work, but pencils are better when teachers ask you to change your answers. In the evenings, some students will stay with local [17]families[/17], while others will sleep in the school building. If you have any problems with your room, speak to me before dinner. The school trip this week is on [18]Friday[/18]. We’re going to a small theatre, and the show is easy to understand. Finally, the school café closes at [19]three[/19] o’clock, so buy snacks before then if you want something later. I hope you enjoy your week and make lots of new friends. On Monday morning, everyone will do a short speaking activity, but it is not a test. It only helps teachers put you in the best class. There is free Wi-Fi in most of the building, although it sometimes works badly in the bedrooms. During breaks, please use English as much as possible, even when you are talking to students from your own country. That is the quickest way to improve.  Teachers will be available in the reception area every afternoon if you need advice about homework or places to visit.

questions.explanation: The speaker says the extra class this week is French.
The speaker says afternoon activities usually start in the library.
The speaker says students should bring a pencil to every lesson.
The speaker says some students will stay with local families.
The speaker says the school trip is on Friday.
The speaker says the school café closes at three o'clock.
